lesson.title: Lesson 60: Too Early and Too Late

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Lesson 60.

translation.text: 第60课。

segments.text: Too Early and Too Late.

translation.text: 太早和太晚。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why did the young girl miss the train?

translation.text: 那个年轻姑娘为什么错过了火车？

segments.text: Punctuality is a necessary habit in all public affairs in civilized society.

translation.text: 在文明社会的一切公共事务中，守时是一种必不可少的习惯。

segments.text: Without it, nothing could ever be brought to a conclusion.

translation.text: 没有守时，任何事情都不可能完成。

segments.text: Everything would be in a state of chaos.

translation.text: 一切都会陷入混乱。

segments.text: Only in a sparsely populated rural community is it possible to disregard it.

translation.text: 只有在人烟稀少的乡村社区，才可能不把守时当回事。

segments.text: In ordinary living, there can be some tolerance of unpunctuality.

translation.text: 在日常生活中，人们可以对不守时稍加宽容。

segments.text: The intellectual, who is working on some abstruse problem, has everything coordinated and organized for the matter in hand.

translation.text: 一位正在研究某个深奥问题的知识分子，会把一切都围绕手头的工作协调和安排好。

segments.text: He is therefore forgiven if late for a dinner party.

translation.text: 因此，即使他赴晚宴迟到，也会得到原谅。

segments.text: But people are often reproached for unpunctuality when their only fault is cutting things fine.

translation.text: 然而，人们常因不守时而受到责备，其实他们唯一的过错只是把时间卡得太紧。

segments.text: It is hard for energetic, quick-minded people to waste time, so they are often tempted to finish a job before setting out to keep an appointment.

translation.text: 精力充沛、头脑敏捷的人很难忍受浪费时间，所以常想在出发赴约之前把一项工作做完。

segments.text: If no accidents occur on the way, like punctured tires, diversions of traffic, descent of fog, they will be on time.

translation.text: 如果路上没有发生轮胎爆裂、交通改道或大雾降临之类的意外，他们便能准时到达。

segments.text: They are often more industrious, useful citizens than those who are never late.

translation.text: 他们往往比那些从不迟到的人更加勤奋，对社会更有用。

segments.text: The over-punctual can be as much a trial to others as the unpunctual.

translation.text: 过分守时的人给别人带来的麻烦，可能不亚于不守时的人。

segments.text: The guest who arrives half an hour too soon is the greatest nuisance.

translation.text: 提前半小时到达的客人最令人头疼。

segments.text: Some friends of my family had this irritating habit.

translation.text: 我家有些朋友就有这种让人恼火的习惯。

segments.text: The only thing to do was ask them to come half an hour later than the other guests.

translation.text: 唯一的办法就是请他们比其他客人晚半小时来。

segments.text: Then they arrived just when we wanted them.

translation.text: 这样，他们就会恰好在我们希望的时间到达。

segments.text: If you are catching a train, it is always better to be comfortably early than even a fraction of a minute too late.

translation.text: 如果要赶火车，宁可从容地早到，也不要迟到哪怕一丁点儿。

segments.text: Although being early may mean wasting a little time, this will be less than if you miss the train and have to wait an hour or more for the next one, and you avoid the frustration of arriving at the very moment when the train is drawing

translation.text: 早到虽然可能浪费一点时间，却比误车后再等一小时甚至更久所浪费的时间少；而且还可避免这样的懊恼：你赶到时火车正缓缓

segments.text: out of the station and being unable to get on it.

translation.text: 驶出车站，你却上不去了。

segments.text: An even harder situation is to be on the platform in good time for a train and still to see it go off without you.

translation.text: 更难受的情况是，你明明及时站在站台上，却仍眼看着火车开走而没能上车。

segments.text: Such an experience befell a certain young girl the first time she was traveling alone.

translation.text: 有个年轻姑娘第一次独自旅行时，就遇到了这样的事。

segments.text: She entered the station twenty minutes before the train was due, since her parents had impressed upon her that it would be unforgivable to miss it and cause the friends with whom she was going to stay to make two journeys to meet her.

translation.text: 她在列车预定到站前二十分钟进了车站，因为父母一再告诫她，误了车而让接待她的朋友跑两趟来接，是不可原谅的。

segments.text: She gave her luggage to a porter and showed him her ticket.

translation.text: 她把行李交给搬运工，并给他看了车票。

segments.text: To her horror, he said that she was two hours too soon.

translation.text: 搬运工却说她早到了两个小时，这令她大吃一惊。

segments.text: She felt in her handbag for the piece of paper on which her father had written down all the details of the journey and gave it to the porter.

translation.text: 她伸手到手提包里，取出父亲写有全部行程细节的那张纸，递给搬运工。

segments.text: He agreed that a train did come into the station at the time on the paper and that it did stop, but only to take on mail, not passengers.

translation.text: 搬运工承认，确实有一列火车会在纸上所写的时间进站，也会停车，但只是装邮件，并不载客。

segments.text: The girl asked to see a timetable, feeling sure that her father could not have made such a mistake.

translation.text: 姑娘要求看列车时刻表，确信父亲不可能犯这样的错误。

segments.text: The porter went to fetch one and arrived back with the station master, who produced it with a flourish, and pointed out a microscopic O beside the time of the arrival of the train at his station.

translation.text: 搬运工去取时刻表，回来时带着站长；站长得意地把表拿出来，指出列车抵达该站的时间旁有一个极小的字母O。

segments.text: This little O indicated that the train only stopped for mail.

translation.text: 这个小小的O表示列车只为装邮件而停靠。

segments.text: Just at that moment the train came into the station.

translation.text: 就在这时，那列火车进站了。

segments.text: The girl, tears streaming down her face, begged to be allowed to slip into the guard's van, but the station master was adamant.

translation.text: 姑娘泪流满面，恳求让她悄悄进入列车员车厢，但站长坚决不肯。

segments.text: Rules could not be broken, and she had to watch that train disappear towards her destination while she was left behind.

translation.text: 规章不能违反；她只好眼看着那列火车驶向自己的目的地，自己却被留在原地。
